Hi, I'm Willa — an illustrator-turned-UX designer currently pursuing my MS in HCDE at the University of Washington. I build warm, human-centered products and craft visual identities — for pet, consumer, and lifestyle brands where storytelling matters.

About me

Where visual
storytelling meets UX

I studied illustration at Ringling College of Art & Design — graduating with honors and winning the Best of Ringling award. That foundation taught me how images carry emotion and guide the eye before a single word is read.

My illustration background means I don't just wireframe — I think about visual rhythm, character, and the feeling a product leaves behind. Most UX designers can't do that.

I'm currently pursuing my Master's in Human Centered Design & Engineering (HCDE) at the University of Washington, where I bring together my background in illustration and visual storytelling with user research, interaction design, and systems thinking. Along the way, I've worked on projects ranging from campus wellness systems to AI edtech usability research, exploring how design can create experiences that are both functional and deeply human.

Selected Work

Pet Care App Design Concept Project End-to-end UX
PawPal — Pet Separation Anxiety App

A self-initiated UX concept addressing the emotional gap between pet owners and their pets. Full process: 50-person survey, 3 interviews, persona, journey map, IA, lo-fi, hi-fi, design system, usability testing.

100% task completion · 100% found it helpful for separation anxiety
UW HCDE Space + Digital Design Team Project
TRU Wellness — Campus Micro-Recovery Pods

Designed "Harmony" biophilic wellness pods for UW students, combining physical pod design with a full digital booking app and kiosk interface. 28-page design specification delivered.

Surveys · Interviews · Physical + Digital prototyping · Usability testing
UW HCDE Usability Research Ed-Tech Client Work
Colleague AI — Usability Evaluation

Conducted in-depth usability testing for a North American ed-tech startup. Identified 10+ critical interaction issues across 3 features using severity rankings, delivered a 40-page professional research report.

5 teachers tested · 10+ issues classified · Actionable redesign recommendations
Illustration Print Design Client Work
Poster, Invitation & Program Series

A cohesive suite of print materials — posters, event invitations, and programs — unified by a custom illustration and typographic system.
Illustration Character Design Client Work
Murder Mystery Character Cards & Covers

Original character illustrations and cover art for a murder mystery game — each card establishing distinct personality through costume, expression, and color.
Illustration Poster Series
Sarasota Aquarium Coral Poster Series

A three-poster series on coral reef ecosystems and marine conservation created for the Sarasota Aquarium, merging scientific illustration with editorial graphic design.
Illustration Thesis
Undergraduate Thesis

A sustained body of illustration work developed through research, iterative concept development, and studio refinement — presented as a cohesive senior thesis exhibition.
